title: IPv6 and Modern Programmable Networks · Operations Brief: Net Ipv6 Transition
summary: Extend the branch design for IPv6, centralized policy, overlays, zero trust, SASE, and infrastructure as code. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net ipv6 transition increment in a dual-stack and programmable-network transition plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose dual stack, tunneling, or NAT64 from application compatibility, operations, observability, and deprecation needs.
Explain SLAAC, router advertisements, DHCPv6 choices, neighbor discovery, and default-gateway learning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Predict Net Ipv6 Transition
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: network ipv6 modern. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: network ipv6 modern. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Net Ipv6 Transition contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose dual stack, tunneling, or nat64 from application compatibility, operations, observability, and deprecation needs.

content.text: Choose dual stack, tunneling, or NAT64 from application compatibility, operations, observability, and deprecation needs. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network ipv6 modern. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Ipv6 Transition, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a dual-stack and programmable-network transition plan while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: NAT64 allows any IPv6-only application to reach any IPv4 service transparently. This fails the Net Ipv6 Transition contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Net Ipv6 Transition, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Build Net Ipv6 Transition evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-ipv6-transition-1xgrcd" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Ipv6 Transition into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Defend the Net Ipv6 Transition model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Ipv6 Transition applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose dual stack, tunneling, or NAT64 from application compatibility, operations, observability, and deprecation needs.
